headline: Water Infrastructure Losses Hit RM7.2 Billion Every Year
context: Non-revenue water losses across Malaysia reached 36.4% translating to RM7.2 billion in lost treated water annually.

cards.big: Malaysia loses RM7.2 billion in treated water annually from 36.4% NRW
cards.sub: Pipe leaks and theft account for over one-third of all treated water

cards.big: Selangor NRW at 31% means 580 million litres leak from pipes daily
cards.sub: Air Selangor replaced only 280km of 32,000km pipe network in 2025

cards.big: Kelantan has worst NRW at 62% losing nearly two-thirds of treated water
cards.sub: State water operator RM1.8 billion in debt cannot fund replacement

cards.big: Japan reduced NRW to 8% through systematic pipe replacement over 30 years
cards.sub: Malaysia would need RM45 billion investment to achieve similar results

cards.big: Water losses test whether infrastructure maintenance receives the same attention as construction
RM7.2 billion in water losses yearly. Pipe replacement pays for itself; the obstacle is institutional inertia.
